The text mentions that the Dutch honored the saint with a yearly festival on December 6th, not December 25th. So A is incorrect. Choice (B) says "Santa Claus" was a misunderstanding of another term. The text explains that "Saint Nikolas" was spoken quickly by the Dutch and sounded like "sinterklaas," which the English said as "Santa Claus." This seems to be a case of mishearing or misunderstanding the original term. So B is a possible correct answer. Choice (C) says it was the Dutch who created Santa Claus. The text explains that the Dutch contributed to the name "Santa Claus" through their pronunciation of "Saint Nikolas," but it doesn't say they created Santa Claus. The saint itself, Saint Nicholas, is mentioned with traditions that the Dutch adapted, but the text doesn't state creation. So C might not be correct.
